from: Vance Holroyd, Senior Asset Officer (Cloud City)
introSubject: RE: CONTRACTOR COHORT INVITATION — Q4 PORTFOLIO REVIEW, JOVIAN SOCIETY
introBody: I trust this finds you well.

My name is Vance Holroyd. I serve as Senior Asset Officer for the Jovian Society, operating out of our Cloud City offices. I am informed your activity record has been reviewed at the asset-strategy level, and I am pleased to extend a formal cohort invitation on the Society's behalf.

The Society is conducting Q4 portfolio review across a select inventory of candidate bodies in the Jovian and Saturn co-orbital regions. The work encompasses photometric assessment, Dynamic Albedo of Neutrons subsurface survey, and prospectus compilation. I'm told your throughput and field quality are both above the median for the contractor pool we typically engage at this tier. We would like to route this work to you.

The engagement breaks into nine deliverables, sequenced for instrumentation onboarding. Compensation is structured per step, with the closeout figure calibrated to the portfolio significance of the final prospectus. Successful completion places the contractor on our preferred manifest — a standing the Society maintains in good faith and, I am told, with meaningful longitudinal benefit.

Please review and confirm acceptance at your convenience. The cohort window is open through end of quarter. The Society operates under standard non-disclosure terms throughout the engagement; acceptance is understood as acknowledgment of those terms.
I look forward to working with you.

steps.subject: OP 1 — BASELINE ASSESSMENT, SURFACE GATHER
flavor: I trust this finds you well.
Welcome to the cohort.
Briefing

The first deliverable is intentionally light. The Society requires a baseline read on field methodology before routing specialty instrumentation work. Please select any Society-listed asteroid contract under the surface-gather classification from our Cloud City kiosk, complete it cleanly, and file the receipt.
Closeout
Standard rates apply on close. The Society pays on receipt.
— Vance Holroyd, Senior Asset Officer

steps.subject: OP 2 — BASELINE ASSESSMENT, BELT OPERATIONS
flavor: I trust this finds you well.
Briefing

The second deliverable covers shuttle-grade extraction. Our underwriting partners require demonstrated turret-operation competency before the Society routes instrumentation work at the specialty tier. Please select any Society-listed belt mining contract from our board and complete it.

A note: if your shuttle is not yet turret-equipped, I would be pleased to connect you with the Engineering bay, Jupiter spaceport. Manifold certification for turret mounting is a prerequisite, and our procurement team maintains relationships that can accelerate the process. Please advise if assistance is needed.
One contract. Clean closeout. You know the rhythm.
— Vance Holroyd, Senior Asset Officer

steps.subject: OP 3 — INSTRUMENTATION CALIBRATION, PHOTOMETRY SERIES
flavor: I trust this finds you well.
Briefing

You are cleared for specialty work. Before the Society routes photometry contracts to your file, our Instrumentation Bay requires a calibration unit to be shipped to your assigned lander. This is standard procedure for the photometry series.

Calibration draws on what our research division designates 'stabilized psychosphere residue' — a trace material recoverable from viroid contacts during ordinary combat or rescue contracts. I'm told the material is harmless in handled quantities. Three units will suffice for photometry instrumentation.

The Society prices this acquisition cost into contractor compensation. Gather the material at your convenience across whatever combat or rescue work fits your schedule, and deliver three units to any Cloud City intake bay when ready.
— Vance Holroyd, Senior Asset Officer

steps.subject: OP 4 — PHOTOMETRIC PASS, ASSET 2306-J
flavor: I trust this finds you well.
Briefing
Calibration registers green. You are cleared for photometry tasking.

First assignment: a candidate body in the Jovian Trojans, currently designated 'preliminary review' in the Society's portfolio. We are calling it Asset 2306-J for ledger purposes. The body will be marked on your navigation system momentarily.
Single photometric pass, standard deliverable. The Society values clean telemetry; please prioritize signal quality over transit speed. Pick the listing from our board and run it.
— Vance Holroyd, Senior Asset Officer

steps.subject: OP 5 — PHOTOMETRIC PASS, ASSET 2306-S
flavor: I trust this finds you well.
Briefing

The Jovian pass returned strong telemetry. The Society would now like to route you to a sister asset in the Saturn co-orbital region. I am aware this is slightly outside our standard operating envelope; the portfolio review is system-wide this quarter and we are — I will be candid — pleased with the quality of your work.
Travel premium is included in the line item. The Society would prefer you not cite the figure to other cohort contractors.
Same instrumentation, same protocol. Asset 2306-S. Single photometric pass.
— Vance Holroyd, Senior Asset Officer

steps.subject: OP 6 — INSTRUMENTATION RECALIBRATION, DAN SERIES
flavor: I trust this finds you well.
Briefing

The Society is advancing your file to the next instrumentation tier. Dynamic Albedo of Neutrons surveys provide the subsurface complement to the photometry work — surface composition is one variable; buried volatiles and lattice structure are quite another. A full prospectus on a candidate body is considerably stronger with both data sets.

DAN instrumentation requires substantially more psychosphere mass than the photometry calibration — eight units, per the manual. I'm told this recurring acquisition request is not the Society's most popular feature. The Society apologizes for the inconvenience and reminds contractors that combat and rescue contracts pay their own freight.
Source the material at your convenience. Deliver eight units to any Cloud City intake bay when ready.
— Vance Holroyd, Senior Asset Officer

steps.subject: OP 7 — SUBSURFACE SURVEY, ASSET 2306-J
flavor: I trust this finds you well.
Briefing
The DAN instrument has been shipped and registered to your lander. First subsurface pass: the Jovian Trojan candidate from the photometry series — Asset 2306-J, familiar territory.

Park in the crater the Society marked during your earlier visit, engage science mode, and run the neutron pulse. The instrumentation team notes that the pulse occasionally registers ambient disturbance during operation near certain body types. I am told this is sensor cross-talk and presents no hazard to the survey. Please complete the pass regardless.
The Society looks forward to reviewing the data.
— Vance Holroyd, Senior Asset Officer

steps.subject: OP 8 — SUBSURFACE SURVEY, ASSET 2306-S
flavor: I trust this finds you well.
Briefing
Final survey deliverable. The Saturn co-orbital body — Asset 2306-S, the same asset you photometry'd in OP 5. DAN protocol, same procedure as the Jovian pass.

I will note that several cohort contractors have reported elevated ambient disturbance during subsurface passes near gas-giant co-orbitals. The instrumentation team continues to assess. I'm told you are cleared to proceed at your discretion; please note any telemetry anomalies in the delivery record rather than stopping the pass.
Travel premium applies on this leg as well. Bring the data home and we will begin compiling the full prospectus.
— Vance Holroyd, Senior Asset Officer

steps.subject: OP 9 — PROSPECTUS COMPILATION AND TRANSMISSION
flavor: I trust this finds you well.
Briefing

Eight deliverables returned clean. The Society is genuinely grateful for the quality of data you have brought back across both instrumentation series. It is, I will say, above what we typically see at this tier.

Final assignment: please travel to Asset 2306-J in the Jovian Trojans. The Society has provisioned a terminal on the surface, near your previous landing zone. Approach the terminal, review the assembled prospectus — your telemetry readings, our analysis, and the recommended asset disposition — and confirm transmission to Cloud City Asset Strategy at your discretion.
Recommendation

On receipt of your transmission, the Society will advance Asset 2306-J through the standard extraction confidence bands. The closeout bonus structure attached to this step reflects the asset's classification. There is no further fieldwork after this deliverable.
Closeout
[pause] Welcome, in advance, to the manifest. The Society is family.
— Vance Holroyd, Senior Asset Officer

transmit.completionSubject: COHORT GRADUATION CONFIRMED — WELCOME TO THE MANIFEST
completionBody: I trust this finds you well.
Receipt confirmed. The Society has processed your file for cohort graduation and credited the closeout compensation.

Asset 2306-J has been entered into the extraction queue. Demolition cycles are scheduled within the standard window. By the time you next transit the Jovian Trojans, the body will no longer be there. This is the expected and efficient close of the portfolio cycle.

In recognition of your service, the Society's Field Engineering division has advanced your shuttle to Cohort Standard. Performance across all systems has been uplifted to reflect Society-grade tasking parameters. We expect to see you on further contracts — at elevated rates — as the portfolio year continues.
It has been a pleasure working with you.
Asset Strategy has flagged a discrepancy in the transmitted prospectus. Cross-reference against raw telemetry confirms the DAN readings were materially altered prior to transmission.

The Society does not pursue formal action at this time. Prior step closeouts stand at standard rates. Your file has been removed from the cohort manifest, effective immediately. We will not be retaining you for further work.
For the record: the Society's analysts do not concur with your determination regarding Asset 2306-J. The body remains under independent review pending an alternate assessment.
We wish you well in your future endeavors.
